description:
GOAL: Wire types.rs's tuple_clause_empty / list_clause_empty / func_clause_empty / map_clause_empty into compile diagnostics. Detect case clauses, multi-clause fn arms, and pattern matches whose pattern intersects empty with the inferred input type — emit a warning so the user sees unreachable code at compile time.

## Why this is atomic

Before: unreachable arms compile silently. After: a single .11.24.6 commit adds a 'warnings' surface to CompiledModule and detects the obvious cases via the existing DNF emptiness machinery. No runtime behavior change; existing tests pass byte-for-byte.

## Deliverables

src/ir_codegen.rs (or src/ir_typer.rs):
- New `Diagnostic { fn_name: String, kind: DiagKind, message: String }` type.
- `CompiledModule.warnings: Vec<Diagnostic>` field.
- After typing, walk multi-clause fns (visible via Module's fn-name groupings) and per-fn block-pattern shapes; for each lowered clause, check if its pattern Descr intersected with the call-site Descr is empty. If empty, emit DiagKind::UnreachableArm.
- Same for case-style chained Ifs (already in IR after lowering): check the predicate's narrowing in each branch against the env Descr.
- Stable sort of diagnostics so output is deterministic.
- New API `CompiledModule::warnings(&self) -> &[Diagnostic]` and `fz run` prints them to stderr.

## Tests

- A clause `fn f(0), do: ...` followed by `fn f(0), do: ...` produces a warning on the second clause (pattern Descr int_lit(0) intersected with itself non-empty, but combined with negation of preceding clause is empty).
- A fn whose param is typed Atom but has an Int-pattern clause: warning UnreachableArm.
- Existing happy-path programs produce zero warnings.

## Out of scope

- Errors (vs warnings) for unreachable arms — choice of severity is policy; warnings are conservative.
- Inhabitedness checks for return types.
- Deep guard-condition narrowing (only direct predicates handled).

## Dependencies

- BLOCKED BY: fz-ul4.11.24.3.
- BLOCKS: none.
title: Exhaustiveness / dead-arm warnings via DNF emptiness
